documentation.readme: A cross-platform, collaborative, Command & Control framework written in C#, designed for red teaming and ease of use
usage_sections: Installation: Docker 1. Install Docker and Docker Compose 2. Run docker compose up -d - Optionally, provide HARDHATADMINUSERNAME and/or HARDHATADMINPASSWORD as environment variables; if omitted, the default admin username and randomly generated password will be written to the teamserver logs o

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, ble_4_x] in the c2 category. Fits when the engagement needs: A cross-platform, collaborative, Command & Control framework written in C#, designed for red teaming and ease of use.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A cross-platform, collaborative, Command & Control framework written in C#, designed for red teaming and ease of use.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `CrucibleC2` from its upstream repository, then run `CrucibleC2 --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. A cross-platform, collaborative, Command & Control framework written in C#, designed for red teaming and ease of use.

name: CrucibleC2
summary: A cross-platform, collaborative, Command & Control framework written in C#, designed for red teaming and ease of use

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need c2, use CrucibleC2 because A cross-platform, collaborative, Command & Control framework written in C#, designed for red teaming and ease of use.
When chaining c2 tools, pair CrucibleC2 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `CrucibleC2 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
